meaning: n.鐢熷瓨鑳藉姏,鍙戣偛鑳藉姏
examples: That is exactly why a more sensible preservation strategy would be to assist the farmers to modernize their farms to the extent needed to maintain viability.
The continued viability of the kiva itself in Spanish mission settlements has also been underestimated by historians.
The pollen viability of Miscanthus sinensis was determined by in vitro pollen germination method.
At issue is the viability of hundreds of thousands of patents and the products behind them.
It has provided assurances that the reforms will not undermine the financial viability of housing associations.
